findings.message: ItemRead is the API response schema (used as response_model for both GET /items and POST /items), and now includes owner_email. This echoes owner email PII to external clients on every read, directly contradicting the data model's own warning in models.py: 'PII: owner email address — do not include in logs or external responses.'
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from the ItemRead response schema so the public API contract does not expose the owner's email. If a client genuinely needs contact info, gate it behind an authorized/internal-only schema and access control rather than the default item read.

findings.message: ItemCreate collects owner_email from request payloads, but create_item(session, payload.name) never uses or persists it — the PII is collected with no stated purpose served (data minimization violation). The endpoint docstring ('Create a new item with optional owner email') is also misleading since the email is silently dropped.
findings.suggestion: Drop owner_email from ItemCreate unless there is a defined, necessary purpose for storing it. If it is needed, persist it explicitly and document the purpose, retention, and access restrictions; otherwise do not accept it at all.
